display.purpose: Hero h1 only; Cormorant Garamond with a Quartz Rose italic accent span.

headline.purpose: Section h2; Cormorant with one rose italic accent per section.

title.purpose: Card titles and FAQ summaries; Cormorant 26-34px.

body.purpose: Geist prose; lede 21px, card body 15px, FAQ 16px. ss01/cv11 + tabular nums on.

label.purpose: Geist Mono uppercase micro-labels — the instrument-readout voice.

code.purpose: Geist Mono code frames and architecture chips, with syntax colors.

shadows.name: glass-rest
shadows.purpose: Default Liquid Glass card / frame at rest.

shadows.name: glass-hover
shadows.purpose: Glass card on hover, paired with translateY(-4px).

shadows.name: nav-glass
shadows.purpose: Sticky nav pill, under a 28px backdrop blur.

shadows.name: button-primary-hover
shadows.purpose: Primary button hover — Aurora Blue glow.

shadows.name: button-secondary-hover
shadows.purpose: Secondary button hover — Quartz Rose glow.

motion.name: ease-glass
motion.purpose: Default 0.15-0.25s easing for hover lifts and glass state transitions.

motion.name: ease-climb
motion.purpose: Preloader mark draw-on and decisive entrances.

motion.name: ease-assemble
motion.purpose: Architecture figure assembling bottom-up on scroll.

motion.name: aurora-drift
motion.purpose: Page-wide aurora bloom drift; disabled under reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Brightest, most-resolved control on the page; near-white glass with an Aurora Blue hover glow.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 13px 22px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #06070b; border: none; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.95) 0%, rgba(240,243,247,0.92) 100%); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(180%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(180%); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.9), inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.06), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.18), 0 10px 32px -8px rgba(91,206,250,0.25), 0 4px 12px -4px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); transition: all 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary .ds-arrow { transition: transform 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,1), inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.24), 0 14px 40px -8px rgba(91,206,250,0.45), 0 6px 16px -4px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:hover .ds-arrow { transform: translateX(4px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #5BCEFA; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Dark glass alternative action; text and ring shift to Quartz Rose on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 13px 22px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #f0f3f7; border: none; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.10) 0%, rgba(255,255,255,0.03) 100%), rgba(10,12,18,0.40); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(180%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(180%); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.16), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.08), 0 10px 30px -10px rgba(0,0,0,0.55); transition: all 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary .ds-arrow { transition: transform 0.2s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); color: #F5A9B8; background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(245,169,184,0.12) 0%, rgba(245,169,184,0.04) 100%), rgba(10,12,18,0.40); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.20), 0 0 0 1px rgba(245,169,184,0.25), 0 14px 40px -8px rgba(245,169,184,0.45); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover .ds-arrow { transform: translateX(4px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F5A9B8; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: The core Liquid Glass surface — translucent dark panel with lit edge, ambient drop, and a 4px hover lift.
components.html:
/ EXECUTION

The EVM, made parallel.

Contract calls execute concurrently, so your transaction doesn't queue behind everyone else's.
components.css: .ds-card { display: block; padding: 44px; border-radius: 24px; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.06) 0%, rgba(255,255,255,0.015) 60%, rgba(255,255,255,0) 100%), rgba(10,12,18,0.50); backdrop-filter: blur(24px) saturate(170%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(24px) saturate(170%); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.10), inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.02), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.05), 0 24px 60px -16px rgba(0,0,0,0.50); transition: transform 0.25s ease, box-shadow 0.25s ease; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.14), inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.02), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.08), 0 32px 80px -16px rgba(0,0,0,0.60); } .ds-card-tag { font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #5BCEFA; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-size: 32px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: -0.015em; line-height: 1.05; margin: 16px 0; color: #f0f3f7; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.55; color: #8b96a8; max-width: 50ch; margin: 0; }

components.name: Spec Chip
components.description: Mono spec token used in the architecture diagram; bordered, nowrap.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1; color: #8b96a8; padding: 8px 11px; border: 1px solid #262b39; border-radius: 8px; background: #06070b; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Key Chip
components.description: Load-bearing component marker — the only sanctioned blue+rose adjacency, via a gradient border.
components.css: .ds-chip-key { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1; color: #f0f3f7; padding: 8px 11px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 8px; white-space: nowrap; background: linear-gradient(#0c0e14, #0c0e14) padding-box, linear-gradient(105deg, #5BCEFA, #F5A9B8) border-box; } .ds-chip-key::before { content: '✦'; margin-right: 7px; font-size: 9px; vertical-align: 1px; color: #5BCEFA; }

components.name: FAQ Disclosure
components.description: Native details/summary; the mono + marker rotates 45deg to a x and shifts blue to rose on open.
components.html:
Is this live on mainnet today?

No. Components ship across phased testnet milestones; the site says so plainly.
components.css: .ds-faq { border-bottom: 1px solid #1a1e29; padding: 28px 0; } .ds-faq-summary { cursor: pointer; list-style: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 32px; font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-size: 26px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #f0f3f7; } .ds-faq-summary::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; } .ds-faq-summary::after { content: '+'; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 22px; color: #5BCEFA; transition: transform 0.2s, color 0.2s; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-faq[open] .ds-faq-summary::after { transform: rotate(45deg); color: #F5A9B8; } .ds-faq-body { font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; color: #8b96a8; margin-top: 16px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.65; max-width: 72ch; }

components.name: Section Kicker
components.description: Mono uppercase micro-label — the instrument-readout voice that tags sections and specs.
components.css: .ds-kicker { font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #5BCEFA; }

narrative.overview: MetaFlux looks the way a precision instrument feels: a near-black machined housing, faces of liquid glass, and one quiet aurora of light behind it all. The system pairs the elegance of a Cormorant Garamond display serif with the exactness of Geist Mono, so every screen reads as measured. Confidence is expressed through restraint and specificity, never through volume. The surface is a single continuous dark field lit from within by a slow-drifting aurora, over which components are built as Liquid Glass: translucent panels with a hairline ring, an inner top highlight, a deep ambient drop shadow, and a colored glow that appears only on interaction. This system rejects crypto-hype grammar, the gradient-and-cards SaaS template, and any motion that fights legibility. Where competitors perform, MetaFlux demonstrates.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, single-field canvas (#06070b) under one page-wide animated aurora; no section seams.
Serif-display + mono pairing: Cormorant Garamond for headings, Geist Mono for every technical label, Geist for body.
Liquid Glass panels everywhere — hairline ring + inner highlight + ambient drop + interaction-only colored glow.
Accent restraint: Aurora Blue and Quartz Rose alternate as structural signals; white is the third, rarest voice.
Motion is choreographed but reduced-motion-safe; effects serve comprehension, not spectacle.

rules.name: The Quiet Signal Rule
rules.body: The aurora's palette is identity, not message. Never label it, explain it, or build copy around it. Its meaning is carried by presence alone.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Blue and Rose are a call-and-response, not interchangeable tints. They alternate deliberately and never wash a single surface together; never introduce a third accent hue.

rules.name: The Rarity Rule
rules.body: Pure white as an accent appears on a deliberately small fraction of any screen. Its scarcity makes it read as the most resolved point.

rules.name: The Serif-Says, Mono-Proves Rule
rules.body: Claims and prose are set in Cormorant; anything that is a fact, spec, label, or value is set in Geist Mono.

rules.name: The Italic-Accent Rule
rules.body: Emphasis inside display headings is a Quartz Rose italic serif span — never bold, color-only, or underline. One per heading.

rules.name: The Lit-Edge Rule
rules.body: Every glass surface must carry the inner top highlight; without it the panel reads as a flat dark box, not glass.

rules.name: The Glow-On-Touch Rule
rules.body: Colored shadow is an interaction reward, never a resting state. At rest the ambient shadow is neutral black.

rules.name: The Bordered-Diagram Exception
rules.body: The architecture stack is not glass: solid raised surfaces with a 1px hairline border. Schematic content earns a schematic treatment.

dos: Do keep the page a single dark field (#06070b) with one page-wide aurora behind everything; let surfaces float seamlessly.
Do build every floating surface as full Liquid Glass — ring + inner top highlight + ambient drop + 20-28px backdrop blur.
Do set claims in Cormorant and facts/labels/values in Geist Mono.
Do alternate Aurora Blue and Quartz Rose as a deliberate call-and-response; reserve Pure White for the single most resolved point.
Do make emphasis with a rose italic serif span, never bold or underline.
Do reserve colored glow for hover/focus only, and ship a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation.
Do verify Ink Muted (#8b96a8) and Ink Dim (#5a6273) clear contrast on whatever surface they sit over; favor Ink for meaningful text.
donts: Don't reach for crypto-hype grammar: no neon-casino glow, rocket/moon iconography, APY-bait numbers, or airdrop urgency.
Don't ship the gradient-and-cards SaaS template, the hero-metric template, or identical icon-heading-text card grids.
Don't over-animate or let any effect fight legibility; motion serves comprehension or it doesn't ship.
Don't explain, caption, or sloganize the aurora palette.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text); emphasis comes from the rose italic serif, weight, or size.
Don't treat glass as decoration on a light or flat surface; it is a committed structural language on a dark field, or it isn't used.
Don't introduce colored left-stripe accent borders >1px (the legacy .legal .view rose stripe is the one pre-existing exception).
Don't add a third accent hue or wash a single surface in both blue and rose at once (the gradient-bordered key chip is the only sanctioned adjacency).
